{{Quote|Morteos was responsible for the death of Republic General Crix Masst on Sinded. Masst was killed by a bomb that was wired into his personal speeder's repulsorlift generator. Morteos was nearly shot down by Y-wing patrol vessels while trying to escape Sinded, but his modified freighter was quick enough to outpace the aging starfighters.|Airen Cracken, ''Wanted by Cracken''|<ref name="WbC" />}}
'''Sinded''' was a terrestrial astronomical object<ref name="WbC" /> located in the [[Sinded system/Legends|Sinded system]] of the [[D'Aelgoth sector/Legends|D'Aelgoth sector]],<ref name="EAOC" /> a part of the [[Western Reaches/Legends|Western Reaches]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]].<ref name="EAOC" /> By [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]],<ref name="Date">[[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']] is dated to three [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates to [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]. Therefore, the events of ''Wanted by Cracken'' translate to [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]].</ref> [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[General/Legends|General]] [[Crix Masst]] was present on Sinded. While there, the [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]] [[Morteos]] wired a bomb into the [[Repulsorlift generator/Legends|repulsorlift generator]] of Masst's personal [[Speeder/Legends|speeder]], which resulted in the [[Death/Legends|death]] of the general. When Morteos attempted to escape Sinded aboard his modified [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]], [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wing starfighters]] on patrol opened fire, nearly shooting the assassin's [[Starship/Legends|vessel]] down. Morteos was ultimately able to escape, however. New Republic General [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]] later mentioned the assassination on Sinded in an entry of the [[Datafile/Legends|datafile]] ''[[Wanted by Cracken]]'' dedicated to Morteos.<ref name="WbC" />

==Behind the scenes==
Sinded was first mentioned in the [[1993]] ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' supplement [[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']], which was written by [[Louis J. Prosperi]] for [[West End Games]].<ref name="WbC" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Sinded [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the celestial body, in grid square L-17.<ref name="TEA" />
